---
title: "r_squared"
generated: true
description: "Per-element $R^2$ (Gaussian) or deviance explained (general)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.glm`](../nitrix-stats-glm.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:r_squared -->
```python
r_squared(result: GLMResult) -> Float[Array, 'V']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:r_squared -->
Per-element $R^2$ (Gaussian) or deviance explained (general).

For the Gaussian family the deviance is the residual sum of squares and the
null deviance the total sum of squares, so this is the ordinary coefficient
of determination $R^2$; for other families it is the fraction of
deviance explained, i.e. `deviance_explained`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:r_squared -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `result` | `GLMResult` | — | — | A fitted `GLMResult` supplying the model and null deviances. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `V`. `(V,)` $R^2$ (or deviance explained), one per element.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:r_squared -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `V` | elements of a mass-univariate map — voxels, vertices, or fixels |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
